plan: 1. Create type definitions file `src/domain/types.ts` with strongly-typed interfaces: `Era` (id, label, yearStart, yearEnd, designCues: { typography, colors, layoutPatterns, uiElements }, vocabularyCues: string[]), `Archetype` (id, label, description, compatibleEraIds, compatibleRegionIds), `Region` (id, label, language, tld, currencySymbol, vocabularyOverrides), plus union/enum types for era IDs, region IDs, archetype IDs.

2. Create era dataset file `src/domain/data/eras.ts` exporting a readonly array of 5+ Era records: early-web (1995-1999), portal-era (2000-2006), web2 (2007-2013), startup-minimalism (2014-2019), alt-timeline (1998-2020). Each record includes design cues (typography, colors, layout patterns, UI elements) and vocabulary cues appropriate to the era.

3. Create region dataset file `src/domain/data/regions.ts` exporting a readonly array of 3+ Region records: en-us (United States/English), de-de (Germany/German), en-gb (United Kingdom/English). Each includes language, TLD, currency symbol, and vocabulary overrides (e.g. German translates 'Home' to 'Startseite').

4. Create archetype dataset file `src/domain/data/archetypes.ts` exporting a readonly array of 8+ Archetype records: budget-airline, os-vendor, media-startup, mmorpg-guild, webmail-provider, e-commerce-shop, forum-community, personal-homepage. Each specifies compatible era IDs and region IDs.
5. Create barrel export `src/domain/data/index.ts` re-exporting all datasets and types for clean imports.

6. Create test file `tests/us-006-dataset-definitions.test.ts` with comprehensive tests: verify era count >= 5, archetype count >= 8, region count >= 3, Germany region exists, all era records have yearStart < yearEnd and non-empty designCues/vocabularyCues, all archetype compatibleEraIds reference valid era IDs, all archetype compatibleRegionIds reference valid region IDs, all datasets are readonly/immutable, and type-checking passes.
7. Run `npm run typecheck`, `npm run lint`, and `npm run test` to confirm all checks pass with zero errors.
testing_recommendations: Unit test: verify dataset cardinality (>=5 eras, >=8 archetypes, >=3 regions)
Unit test: validate referential integrity — every compatibleEraId in archetypes maps to a real era, every compatibleRegionId maps to a real region
Unit test: confirm Germany region exists with language 'de' and appropriate TLD/vocabulary overrides
Unit test: each era has valid yearStart < yearEnd, non-empty designCues object, and non-empty vocabularyCues array
Unit test: each archetype has at least one compatible era and one compatible region
Unit test: no duplicate IDs across eras, archetypes, or regions
Typecheck: run `tsc --noEmit` to confirm all datasets satisfy their interfaces with strict mode enabled
Snapshot test: snapshot the full dataset arrays to catch accidental mutations in future changes
risks: Over-engineering vocabulary/design cue structures — keep them simple string arrays or flat objects; downstream consumers (renderer) will define how to use them
Archetype-era compatibility matrix may be too restrictive or too loose — err on the side of broader compatibility since the generator can further filter
German vocabulary overrides need to be plausible, not Google Translate quality — use common web terminology (Startseite, Impressum, Datenschutz, etc.)
TypeScript strict mode with noUncheckedIndexedAccess means lookup helpers must handle undefined — use Map or typed getter functions rather than raw array indexing
Dataset size affects bundle — keep datasets as plain objects (tree-shakeable) and avoid importing unnecessary data at runtime
